channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Ivory Coast votes as Ouattara seeks a fourth term with key rivals sidelined
item.description: ABIDJAN, Ivory Coast (AP) — Voters in Ivory Coast cast ballots Saturday to elect a new leader as longtime President Alassane Ouattara seeks a fourth term while his key contenders were barred from the race.
item.dc:creator: OPE ADETAYO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia faces a shrinking and aging population and tries restrictive laws to combat it
item.description: Russia has grappled with a shrinking and aging population for decades, with President Vladimir Putin viewing it as a threat to national security.
item.dc:creator: Katie Marie Davies, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Europe fights to avoid being sidelined by Trump’s world-shaping plans
item.description: Donald Trump has seized a central role on the global stage, leaving European allies on the sidelines.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Malaysia warns ASEAN neutrality eroding as global powers compete
item.description: Malaysian Foreign Minister Mohamad Hasan has warned Southeast Asian counterparts that neutrality is shrinking due to big power rivalry.
item.dc:creator: Eileen Ng, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Houthi rebels detain 2 more UN staffers in Yemen as the world body reevaluates its operations
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Houthi rebels detained two additional United Nations staff members and raided the homes of several others in Yemen over the last 48 hours, the latest in a series of events now forcing the world body to reassess how it operates in the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and AHMED AL-HAJ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump hits Colombian President Petro with sanctions
item.description: The Trump administration leveled sanctions directly on Colombian President Gustavo Petro and his family members, escalating his feud with the South American leader.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Japan's new leader vows to further boost defense spending as regional tensions rise
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, in her first major policy speech on Friday, pledged to further accelerate Japan’s military buildup and spending and complete an early upgrade of the country’s security strategy as tensions rise with China, North Korea and Russia.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon sends largest aircraft carrier to Caribbean amid boat strikes
item.description: The Pentagon is sending the world’s largest aircraft carrier, USS Gerald R. Ford, and its carrier air wing to the Caribbean as the Trump administration conducts strikes against boats it says are smuggling illegal drugs in the region.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Roadside bombing kills 3 police officers in northwest Pakistan
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — A powerful roadside bomb struck a police vehicle Friday in a former stronghold of the Pakistani Taliban in the country’s northwest near the Afghan border, killing a city police chief and two junior officers, officials said.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How a German freight lift became an unexpected social media star in the Louvre heist
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Forget France: The Louvre Museum jewelry heist was a classic case of German efficiency. Photos of a German-made freight lift that the thieves used in a lightning-fast daylight heist at the Louvre have gone viral. And its manufacturer is now riding high from the unexpected endorsement.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: Canada 'trying to illegally influence' Supreme Court on tariffs
item.description: President Trump defended his decision to call off trade negotiations with Canada on Friday, accusing the U.S.'s northern neighbor of attempting to "illegally influence" a pending Supreme Court case regarding tariffs. "CANADA CHEATED AND GOT CAUGHT!!!" Trump posted to Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Reporter’s Notebook: A Paris jewel heist straight out of the movies
item.description: A movie-style heist at the Louvre saw criminals scale the museum with a cherry picker truck and steal priceless French crown jewelry before fleeing on scooters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy urges US to broaden Russian oil sanctions and seeks long-range missiles
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has urged the United States to expand sanctions on Russia’s two biggest oil companies to the whole sector.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lithuanian president says Russian military planes violated the Baltic country's airspace
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — Russian military planes briefly violated Lithuania’s airspace Thursday evening, the Lithuanian president said, condemning what he called a blatant breach of the territorial integrity of his European Union and NATO-member country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa lumbers through the Caribbean, killing an elderly man in Haiti
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa lumbered through the Caribbean Sea on Thursday, bringing a risk of dangerous landslides and life-threatening flooding to Jamaica and southern Hispaniola — an island shared by the Dominican Republic and Haiti.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin deplores US sanctions as 'unfriendly' while EU joins in heaping restrictions on Russia
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The European Union on Thursday heaped more economic sanctions on Russia, adding to U.S. President Donald Trump’s new punitive measures the previous day against the Russian oil industry.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rubio will travel to Israel to ensure fragile Gaza ceasefire holds
item.description: Secretary of State Rubio plans to visit a new civilian military coordination center in Israel, which was visited by Vice President JD Vance earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: RENATA BRITO and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Woman charged in $1.7M gold heist at Paris museum: Report
item.description: Investigators revealed the woman had left France the day of the break-in and was preparing to return to China.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth says US conducted another strike in Eastern Pacific targeting alleged narco-traffickers
item.description: Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth said on Wednesday that the U.S. military has carried out another strike in the Eastern Pacific targeting alleged narco-terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin directs drills of Russian nuclear forces as his summit with Trump is put on hold
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Wednesday directed drills of the country’s strategic nuclear forces that featured practice missile launches, an exercise that came as his planned summit on Ukraine with U.S. President Donald Trump was put on hold.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump levies new sanctions on Russian oil giants in a push on Putin to end Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration announced Wednesday new “massive sanctions” against Russia's oil industry that are aimed at moving Russian President Vladimir Putin to the negotiating table and bringing an end to Moscow's brutal war on Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, SUSIE BLANN and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Iraq bans Roblox over child safety concerns
item.description: Iraq has joined several countries — including China, Turkey, Oman and Qatar — to ban U.S. user-generated video game platform Roblox over child safety.
item.dc:creator: Cher Hwang, NewsNation

item.title: American missionary kidnapped in Niger by suspected Islamist militants, sources say
item.description: Suspected Islamist militants have kidnapped an American missionary in the West African nation of Niger, U.S. official sources told Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dublin protesters clash with police, burn vehicle after migrant accused of sexually assaulting Irish girl
item.description: Dublin, Ireland, protests turned violent Tuesday and a police car was set ablaze after a migrant allegedly sexually assaulted a girl in state child care.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Louvre burglars obtained truck-mounted lift to make off with jewels worth more than $100M
item.description: Thieves used a stolen truck-mounted moving lift to scale the Louvre and steal royal jewels worth over $100 million in a lightning-fast Paris heist.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Louvre director summoned by lawmakers after jewel theft
item.description: The Louvre is facing questions about lax security measures after thieves made off with priceless jewels.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Kartalija, NewsNation

item.title: Experts urge Trump to ban terror-linked UN agency from his Gaza peace plan
item.description: Mideast experts urge excluding UNRWA from Gaza reconstruction under President Trump's peace plan, citing the agency's alleged Hamas connections and incompetence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexican lime growers' leader found dead after anti-cartel statements
item.description: In interviews, Bernardo Bravo ripped into organized crime's "permanent commercial hijacking of any commercial activity.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Sanae Takaichi becomes Japan’s first female prime minister, citing Thatcher inspiration
item.description: Sanae Takaichi becomes Japan's first female prime minister, citing Margaret Thatcher as inspiration while promising to restore confidence and strengthen security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former French President Sarkozy begins a 5-year prison sentence for campaign finance conspiracy
item.description: Nicolas Sarkozy becomes the first modern French president imprisoned after conviction for criminal conspiracy involving Libya campaign financing in 2007 election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance says fragile Gaza ceasefire plan going better than expected
item.description: The U.S.-brokered deal has wavered amid renewed violence and uncertainty over next steps toward long-term peace.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Dad sends 11-year-old to bed thinking he was drunk; boy later dies of snake bite
item.description: The cause of death for an 11-year-old boy in Australia was confirmed last week by the Coroners Court of Queensland.
item.dc:creator: Laura Morrison, NewsNation

item.title: US envoys visit Israel to bolster truce, and Gaza militants hand over a hostage's remains
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Two of U.S. President Donald Trump’s envoys traveled to Israel on Monday to shore up the ceasefire in Gaza, a day after deadly violence gave the fragile deal its first major test.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Colombia recalls US ambassador after Trump's tariff threat, drug remarks
item.description: Trump also called Colombian's leftist President Gustavo Petro an "illegal drug leader" on Sunday, which Petro's government described as offensive.
item.dc:creator: Luis Jaime Acosta and Julia Symmes Cobb / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Pope meets with global group of clergy abuse survivors to talk zero tolerance
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Leo XIV met Monday for the first time with an organization of clergy abuse survivors and advocates, who said he agreed to maintain a permanent dialogue as they press for a zero-tolerance policy for abuse in the Catholic Church.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: China accuses US of cyberattack on national time center
item.description: China said any damage to related facilities could have disrupted network communications, financial systems and power supply.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Ukraine's Donbas region will have to be 'cut up' to end Russian invasion
item.description: Trump's latest comments came after Ukrainian drones struck a major gas processing plant in southern Russia.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
